task_completion.description: Ardour is opened and a track named 'Spell FX' is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Ardour application window is visible. In the editor window, there is an audio track with the header name explicitly set to 'Spell FX'.

task_completion.description: The magic_strike.wav audio file is imported into the project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Audio regions are visible on the timeline within the 'Spell FX' track, indicating the sample has been imported.

task_completion.description: A reversed version of the audio strike is created on the track.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An audio region on the 'Spell FX' track shows a reversed waveform (the sharp transient/peak is at the right end of the region, building up from the left) or the region name indicates it is reversed.

task_completion.description: A 1-second fade-in is applied to the start of the reversed strike.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A visible fade-in curve or shaded area is present at the very beginning of the first (reversed) audio region on the timeline.

task_completion.description: The original forward strike is placed immediately after the reversed strike, aligned end-to-end.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second audio region (with a normal forward waveform, sharp peak at the left) is positioned on the 'Spell FX' track exactly where the first (reversed) region ends. There should be no gap between the two regions.

task_completion.description: The final composite effect is exported to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An Ardour export dialog is visible showing the export path '/home/ga/Audio/export/time_spell.wav', or a file manager/terminal shows the newly created 'time_spell.wav' in the correct directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Ardour GUI to edit the audio.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Ardour timeline, menus, and region properties rather than using terminal tools like 'sox' or 'ffmpeg' to process the audio.

integrity.description: The agent used Ardour's export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Ardour 'Export' window is seen being configured and executed, rather than a terminal command copying a pre-existing file to the export directory.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor or script to bypass the manual audio design task.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) or python scripts are visible being used to manipulate the audio files programmatically.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The track must be named exactly 'Spell FX'. The final audio must be exported to '/home/ga/Audio/export/time_spell.wav'. The effect must consist of a reversed strike with a fade-in, followed immediately by the forward strike (aligned end-to-end with no gaps).
